messages.content:
4Kids_Editor: Hello, 4Kids fans! Big news! We’re thrilled to announce that starting the first Saturday of 2024, 4Kids will be taking over the CW Saturday morning block! We’ve got a fantastic lineup planned that we can’t wait to share with you. Expect more details in the coming weeks. Stay tuned!

SuperSaiyanGoku22: That’s huge! Congrats to the 4Kids team. Any hints on what shows are going to be part of the lineup? 🤔

ToonFanatic84: With Teen Titans Go being the current titan of Kids WB, it’ll be interesting to see if 4Kids brings any superhero content to the table.

4Kids_Editor: While I can’t reveal specifics just yet, let’s just say fans of action and humor will be very pleased. 😉

RetroRikiFan: Wait, is this a hint that GoGoRiki could play a major role? It has that perfect blend of action and humor, and considering its success elsewhere, it’d be the right fit.

PokeMasterJojo: Speaking of shows, how are things looking on the 4Kids TV channel? Last I checked, Pokémon was doing pretty solid.

4Kids_Editor: Pokémon continues to perform exceptionally well for us – it’s a fantastic franchise with a dedicated fanbase. However, it’s true that One Piece has been facing some challenges in the ratings department. We’re working on strategies to bring more attention to it.

ToonFanatic84: Interesting to hear about One Piece. It seems like anime can be hit or miss outside of Japan. Pokémon is just universally loved, I guess.

AnimeLover101: It’s all about how it’s adapted and presented, IMO. One Piece has a massive following, so maybe it’s more about finding what clicks with the audiences here.

RetroRikiFan: Going back to GoGoRiki, if it’s part of the new lineup, I’m betting it’ll become the most popular show on the block. There’s just something about those characters that could really resonate with viewers, especially if Teen Titans Go’s comedy style is anything to go by.

4Kids_Editor: All wonderful points and speculation! We’re definitely taking note of what our viewers are interested in. More detailed announcements will come soon, so keep an eye out. Thanks for all your excitement and support—2024 is going to be an amazing year for 4Kids on the CW and our 4Kids TV channel!


4Kids_Editor: Hey, 4Kids community! Following up on our previous announcement and all the buzzing theories (shoutout to RetroRikiFan for calling out GoGoRiki 😉), we wanted to touch upon the vibe we’re aiming for with our new CW Saturday morning block.

The world can be a tough place, and we believe in the power of light-hearted, fun programming that can bring smiles to faces, young and old. Shows like GoGoRiki embody that spirit—cute, engaging, and filled with positive messages and laughs. We’re betting big that such shows will not only be very popular but also set a refreshing tone for what kids’ entertainment can be about.

Now, for some digital front news! We’ve seen some interesting shifts in what’s catching fire on 4kids.tv. As of now, here are the four most streamed shows on our platform:

1. Pokémon - No surprise here! Pokémon continues to capture hearts with its endless adventures and beloved characters.
2. Yu-Gi-Oh! - Still a powerhouse in the streaming world. It seems the heart of the cards resonates with quite a lot of you!
3. Sonic X - Speeding up to the third spot, Sonic X has seen a resurgence, proving that the Blue Blur is timeless.
4. Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles - Heroes in a half shell have climbed up the ranks, bringing action and pizza-fueled fun to viewers.

It’s fascinating to see the diverse interests of our audience. From classic battles in Yu-Gi-Oh! to the fast-paced adventures in Sonic X, it’s clear our fans love a wide range of themes and stories.

Given this insight, we’re even more excited about our plans for the new CW block and are committed to bringing a mix of action, humor, and heartfelt stories to the forefront. Shows like GoGoRiki, with its unique charm and positive messages, are exactly what we believe will resonate with our viewers.

What do you think of the current top shows? Any surprises or predictions for what might climb the ranks next year? Let’s hear your thoughts!

And remember, our aim is to light up your Saturdays with joy and adventure. The countdown to 2024 begins - we can’t wait to share this journey with you! 🌟📺

PokeMasterJojo: Super pumped to see Pokémon leading the pack! It truly is a show that never gets old. Also, really curious about how GoGoRiki will do. I remember it being quite fun!

YuGiOhMaster88: Yu-Gi-Oh! holding strong! It’s amazing how the fanbase remains so engaged after all these years. Definitely looking forward to seeing what the new CW block has in store for us.

SpeedsterFan: Sonic X making a comeback is the best news! Shows the classics can still kick it with the best of them. Can’t wait to see what’s next!

TurtlePower: TMNT is where it’s at! Action, humor, and the best band of brothers. Excited to see what else 4Kids has planned if this is the kind of quality they’re aiming for.


4Kids_Blogger: Greetings, 4Kids community! We hope you’re as excited as we are as we countdown to our takeover of the CW Saturday morning block on January 6, 2024. We’re busy preparing a lineup that’s sure to dazzle and entertain, with a mix of fan favorites and fresh faces.

In other news, we’ve been monitoring the performance of various shows across the Kids WB, and it’s become apparent that “Squish” has been facing some significant challenges in capturing the audience’s attention. Despite its creative approach to storytelling and animation, the show hasn’t performed as well as hoped in its current slot.

ToonWorldCritic: I’ve noticed “Squish” struggling too. Honestly, with the kind of ratings it’s been pulling, I wouldn’t be surprised if it gets replaced as soon as 4Kids takes over on Jan 6. It’s a competitive block, and every show needs to pull its weight.

AnimeAdventurer: It’s tough seeing a show not do well, but I’m really looking forward to what 4Kids brings to the table. I have faith that the new lineup will be something special!

SaturdayMorningFan: Sad about “Squish,” but honestly, the anticipation for the new shows 4Kids is planning is through the roof. I’m hoping for a mix of action and comedy—something that both kids and parents can enjoy together!

4Kids_Blogger: We’re grateful for your understanding and enthusiasm! It gives us great confidence knowing we have such a supportive and excited audience as we work towards launching our new lineup. Ensuring a vibrant and engaging Saturday morning experience for all our viewers remains our top priority, and we can’t wait to unveil what we’ve been working on for January 6, 2024. Stay tuned, and thank you for your continued support!

SentriyMoceu: If anything I’d assume one of the comedic shows they have (e.g Viva Pinata, Four Eyes!, GoGoRiki) will replace it. Not sure why GoGoRiki is only available in America, probably because of KikoRiki everywhere else, Anyways, Code Lyoko, Johnny Test and Teen Titans Go are the most rated shows on Kids WB, so it will be interesting to see if they make the cut

Adrenalinieh: Four Eyes! is that acquired show about the alien girl, For those unaware, it revolves around Emma, a alien girl from Albacore 7 who is sent to Earth to repeat 5th grade, She is a pink, four eyed squid in alien form, but when she wears a special set of glasses she becomes human, and the worst part is she is a geek (bucktooth and all), Her only friends are 2 Earth boys named Pete and Skyler, It’s pretty funny, It was originally produced by Porchlight Entertainment and Pictor Media, but when Porchlight shut down the rights became lost untill 2020 when the show was bought by 4Kids, Also I have no idea what’s going on with the CW block yet, although I do know the full lineup is hitting in the Spring (with GoGoRiki, Pokemon, Yu Gi Oh, Turtles, Sonic X and probably Chaotic).

Klusiei: Leaving the lineup is Shaman King and Beyblade Metal Fusion/Fury, being removed from December 1st, replacing them is Pokemon Battle Frontier and Pokemon XYZ (Guess 4Kids really are glad they got the rights back, AND they even can broadcast and stream the seasons TPCI dubbed), I expect then is when we’ll get an announcement on Kids WB changing its name, Also, they recently aired one of the GoGoRiki movies (The Time Team, also known as Smeshariki: Deja Vu). Write a continuation forum convo where the first user (MaxK) takes a guess as to what will replace Squish, and they also worry for Beyblade)
MaxK: Sad to hear about Squish struggling, but with 4Kids taking over in 2024, I’m curious to see what will fill its slot. Given the speculation and love for GoGoRiki, I wouldn’t be too surprised if it or Four Eyes! takes over. But what about Beyblade? With Shaman King and Beyblade Metal Fusion/Fury being pulled off the air, I’m starting to worry about the future of Beyblade on the channel. Any chance it could make a return, or are we looking at a complete refresh of the lineup?

BladeMasterZ: Yeah, the removal of Beyblade has me concerned too. That series has a loyal fanbase, and it would be a shame to see it disappear completely from the lineup. Maybe 4Kids has plans to reintroduce it later on? Would love to see something new or perhaps a return of some classic series in a new slot.

AnimeGalaxy: With all the changes happening, it’s an exciting time for 4Kids and the CW block. I’ve got high hopes for GoGoRiki and Four Eyes!, both seem like they could bring a fresh vibe. As for Beyblade, I’m holding out hope that it’s not gone for good. Maybe they’re making room for something big?

RetroToonFan: Honestly, seeing Pokémon and Yu-Gi-Oh! still going strong gives me hope for other classic series to maintain their spot or make a comeback. Beyblade has such a rich history with 4Kids; I can’t imagine it won’t make some kind of return. Also, super excited for the potential addition of Four Eyes! and GoGoRiki. The lineup sounds like it’s shaping up to be a solid mix of action, adventure, and laughs!

ToonAnalyzer: Remember, 4Kids taking over the CW Saturday morning block could mean they’re looking to revitalize it with a diverse selection of shows. While it’s sad to see beloved shows like Squish and potentially Beyblade not making the cut immediately, it opens the door for new content and revamps of existing franchises. GoGoRiki and Four Eyes! seem like just the beginning. Let’s not forget, in the world of television and entertainment, changes are constant. Who knows? We might see a resurgence of Beyblade in a new format or part of a strategic roll-out later in 2024.

MaxK: Great points all around. Guess it’s a waiting game now to see how everything unfolds. Really hoping for a big comeback for Beyblade in some form. Until then, I’m keeping an open mind about the new lineup. Excited to see how 4Kids will spice things up come January 2024!
Write a continuation about the ratings for the Friday Fun-Noon block on 4Kids TV (which has 5 shows) Chaotic got mid ratings, whereas Pokemon got stellar ratings,
TVTrendWatcher: Speaking of 4Kids’ lineup changes, has anyone been keeping an eye on the Friday Fun-Noon block ratings? Last I checked, Pokémon was absolutely killing it with stellar ratings – proof that the franchise is still a titan in children’s entertainment. But it seems Chaotic isn’t faring quite as well, landing in the mid-range. It’s a cool concept, but maybe it’s not resonating as much with the audience?

CardCollectKid: It’s a bummer about Chaotic. I personally love the blend of card games and storytelling. However, Pokémon reigns supreme not just in gaming but in the TV world, it seems. It just has that magical formula that keeps kids (and let’s be honest, plenty of adults) hooked.

AnimationAfficionado: Besides Pokémon and Chaotic, does anyone know how the other shows in the Friday Fun-Noon block are doing? I heard mixed reviews but haven’t seen any hard data on ratings.

DigitalDynamo: If memory serves, Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles and Sonic X are holding strong with good ratings, especially when there are big story arcs or crossover events. As for the fifth show, Yu-Gi-Oh!, it’s not hitting Pokémon levels but is still a solid performer, appealing to its loyal fanbase with its ever-evolving storyline and high-stakes duels.

RetroToonFan: Makes sense. TMNT and Sonic X have that nostalgic factor for parents and still manage to feel fresh for the new generation. Yu-Gi-Oh! has always had a dedicated following, too. It’s interesting to see how different franchises perform on TV versus their other media, like games or comics.

ToonTownInsider: With Pokémon setting the benchmark so high, I wonder if 4Kids might consider rejigging the Friday Fun-Noon block to boost overall viewership. Maybe introduce a new show to see if it can catch that Pokémon lightning in a bottle?

TVTrendWatcher: That’s a possibility. With the constant evolution of kids’ interests, 4Kids could indeed look for another hit to complement Pokémon. It might also be worthwhile for them to invest more in marketing lesser-watched shows like Chaotic, perhaps by integrating them more with popular social media challenges or interactive online platforms.

CardCollectKid: Agreed. Also, maybe some crossover specials could help? Pokémon’s had a few cameos in other 4Kids shows over the years, which always seemed to spike interest. Imagine a well-done Pokémon and TMNT crossover event?

AnimationAfficionado: A crossover like that could certainly make waves. Maybe throw in some promotional events, too, like limited edition cards for Chaotic or Yu-Gi-Oh! celebrating the event.

DigitalDynamo: Ultimately, it’s all about keeping the content engaging and finding creative ways to draw in viewers. Pokémon’s success shows there’s a huge appetite for compelling stories and beloved characters. If 4Kids plays its cards right, especially with the upcoming changes to the CW Saturday morning block, they might just find another blockbuster to match Pokémon’s appeal.

ToonTownInsider: Keeping the momentum going will be key. With the strategic moves 4Kids is making, both on TV and digital fronts, fans have a lot to look forward to. Here’s hoping the entire Friday Fun-Noon block, not just Pokémon, continues to evolve and entertain in new and exciting ways!
Write a follow up convo where Kids WB’s name is changing to the CW4Kids on January 6, as seen by ads running on the block today, Incidentally no 4Kids shows are seen in the ad, just KWB ones, so the programming isn’t changing just yet
ToonScoopMaster: Hey everyone, did you catch the ads running on Kids WB today? It looks like the name is officially changing to CW4Kids on January 6. But interestingly, the ad didn’t feature any 4Kids-specific shows, just the regular KWB lineup. Seems like the programming isn’t changing just yet.

AnimationInsider: Noticed that too. It’s a bit of a surprise not to see any teases of what’s coming, especially with 4Kids taking over. Maybe they’re planning a separate reveal for the new lineup? Keeping us guessing, I suppose.

RetroToonFan: I find it intriguing. A name change suggests a big shift, yet holding back on new show announcements. It’s like they’re setting the stage for something grand. Still, odd not to hint at anything, especially with the takeover so close.

DigitalDynamo: Agreed. It’s all a bit mysterious. You’d think they’d at least sneak in something about GoGoRiki or Pokémon to hype up the transition. Maybe the strategy is to keep the current audience comfortable with the familiar KWB shows before introducing new ones?

ToonTownInsider: That makes sense. A sudden overhaul could alienate existing viewers. Perhaps they’re opting for a gradual introduction to the 4Kids content. It’s a smart move, easing the audience into the new brand while still respecting the legacy of Kids WB.

AnimationAfficionado: Also, could it be a licensing thing? Maybe they’re waiting until everything is officially rebranded as CW4Kids before they start promoting the 4Kids lineup. Logistics can be tricky with these big changes.

CartoonCritic101: True. I wonder how they’ll manage the transition in terms of content blend. Will it be an equal mix of KWB favorites and new 4Kids shows, or will they lean heavily into their own catalogue? Only time will tell, but it’s definitely an interesting development.

RetroToonFan: For now, we play the waiting game. It’s all speculation until January 6. But I must admit, this has piqued my curiosity more than a straightforward announcement might have. Here’s hoping the reveal lives up to the suspense they’re building.

ToonScoopMaster: Absolutely. The anticipation definitely has the community buzzing, which might be exactly what they intended. Let’s keep an eye out for any sneak peeks or leaks in the meantime. The countdown to CW4Kids begins!
Write a follow up where 4Kids TV begins a movie day on the 3rd of December with 3 Pokemon films and that GoGoRiki one
ToonScoopMaster: Big news for all animation fans! 4Kids TV just announced they’re launching a special movie day on December 3rd featuring not one, not two, but three Pokémon films back-to-back! And to top it all off, they’re also airing that GoGoRiki movie that’s been generating quite a buzz. Seems like a fantastic way to spend a Saturday!

AnimationInsider: Wow, that’s quite a lineup! Pokémon movies have always been a hit, but adding the GoGoRiki movie into the mix is an interesting choice. It shows 4Kids is serious about blending their classic content with new favorites. Can’t wait!

RetroToonFan: This is incredible! Pokémon movies are always an event in themselves, but I’m particularly excited to see how the GoGoRiki movie is received. It’s a smart move by 4Kids TV to introduce the audience to a wider variety of their offerings.

DigitalDynamo: Definitely a strategic play here, showcasing the range of 4Kids TV’s portfolio. Pokémon has a massive, loyal fanbase, and leveraging that to bring attention to GoGoRiki is clever. Curious to see if this leads to more viewers sticking around for GoGoRiki’s regular showtimes.

ToonTownInsider: Does anyone know which Pokémon movies they’ll be showing? The franchise has some real gems, and it could be a great opportunity for 4Kids TV to pull in viewers from all age groups. Pairing that with the GoGoRiki movie is an excellent way to highlight their diverse programming slate.

CartoonCritic101: Great question, @ToonTownInsider. They haven’t released specific titles yet, but I’m hoping for a mix of classics and perhaps something a bit more recent to keep things fresh. Regardless, it’s going to be a Pokémon-packed day, and I’m here for it.

AnimationAfficionado: Agreed. And I’m especially curious about the GoGoRiki movie. It’s an opportunity for it to shine and capture a wider audience. If it’s well-received, this could mean big things for GoGoRiki on 4Kids TV.

RetroToonFan: Absolutely. December 3rd is marked on my calendar. It’s not every day we get such a curated selection of movies on 4Kids TV. Here’s hoping this movie day becomes a regular event. It’s a fantastic way for fans, old and new, to come together and enjoy some quality animation.

ToonScoopMaster: Couldn’t agree more! Let’s make sure to spread the word and get as many eyes on this movie day as possible. It could mean more such events in the future. Stay tuned, folks, and let’s gear up for an epic day of Pokémon and GoGoRiki!
Write a follow up where the Pokemon movies are revealed and thr GoGoRiki one is “Team Invincible” (the one where the gang become superheroes in a city trying to impress a “superhero” who’s really a TV star, and save Chikoriki the Hedgehog)
ToonScoopMaster: Update on the 4Kids TV movie day lineup! The Pokémon movies have been revealed, and they’re gearing up to showcase “Pokémon: The First Movie - Mewtwo Strikes Back”, “Pokémon: The Movie 2000 - The Power of One”, and “Pokémon 3: The Movie - Spell of the Unown”. And for the GoGoRiki feature, it’s confirmed to be “Team Invincible” - yes, the one where our lovable GoGoRiki gang turns into superheroes trying to impress a celebrity, only to embark on a real adventure to save Chikoriki the Hedgehog!

AnimationInsider: Wow, they’re really pulling out the big guns with those Pokémon titles – all classics! And “Team Invincible” is such a fun choice for GoGoRiki. I remember that episode being packed with humor and heart. It’s going to be an awesome blend of nostalgia and new discoveries for viewers.

RetroToonFan: Couldn’t agree more. “Team Invincible” is a great showcase of GoGoRiki’s charm and wit. Coupling that with the epic storytelling of the chosen Pokémon movies? December 3rd can’t come soon enough. It’s the perfect way to introduce newer audiences to GoGoRiki while keeping Pokémon fans glued to their screens.

DigitalDynamo: The selection of Pokémon movies is spot-on for catching a broad audience. Starting with the very first movie and moving through to “Spell of the Unown” is a brilliant strategy to captivate viewers with a mix of action, adventure, and emotion. And “Team Invincible” is just icing on the cake, offering a lighter, comedic counterpoint to the Pokémon drama.

ToonTownInsider: “Team Invincible” is such a memorable GoGoRiki story. The gang’s earnest (and hilarious) attempts at being superheroes to impress a TV star who they think is a real hero is classic GoGoRiki comedy. Plus, it brings a nice message about the real meaning of heroism. Pairing it with those Pokémon movies, which each have their own powerful themes, makes for a compelling lineup indeed.

CartoonCritic101: Agreed on all points. This is a lineup that not only entertains but also delivers messages of courage, friendship, and understanding your own value. Both the Pokémon movies and “Team Invincible” complement each other in that regard. 4Kids TV is setting a high bar for family-friendly programming with this movie day.

AnimationAfficionado: I’m definitely excited for “Team Invincible”. It’s a smart move to introduce GoGoRiki in a way that highlights its unique approach to storytelling and humor right alongside Pokémon’s epic tales. Could really broaden GoGoRiki’s fan base if it hits the right note with the audience.

RetroToonFan: For sure. And let’s not underestimate the power of nostalgia with those Pokémon movies. They’re timeless and have a way of bringing people together. Mixed with the fresh energy of “Team Invincible”, this movie day is shaping up to be a memorable event for 4Kids TV.

ToonScoopMaster: Absolutely! Let’s all make sure to spread the word and support this fantastic lineup. It’s opportunities like these that can really showcase the depth and variety of content 4Kids TV has to offer. Here’s to a successful movie day and hopefully many more to come!

January 27 2024

Should have seen this coming the day TAWOG and COTC left for good, but Kids WB died on December 30, We now have the CW4Kids, owned by 4Kids Entertainment, and the first schedule change has happened, Squish and The Amazing Adrenalini Brothers have been replaced with Pokemon: Indigo League and Sonic X respectively, The block still has the Kids WB aesthetic, but I expect that to be gone by Easter, Also, another schedule change is coming in a few months, Teen Titans Go, Scooby Doo Mystery Inc and Batman the Brave and the Bold are being replaced with GoGoRiki (Smeshariki), Ultimate Muscle and Kirby: Right Back At Ya in 2 weeks, The remaining KWB shows as of now are Johnny Test, Xiaolin Showdown, Code Lyoko, Ultimate Spider Man (and in 2 weeks, Gloopy and Glitter,  For those unaware this show was about 2 gooey blobs, the male one teal colored and named Gloopy, the female one fuchsia, sparkling and named Glitter, They were both aliens, Debuted on WB around September 2022, and was removed in late October). The way they went about this was interesting, In December they started airing promos saying that from January 6 the network would be changing it’s name, it didn’t tell us that 4Kids are now in charge or that there would be new shows, the promo just showed the usual KWB shows like Squish, TAAB, TTG, XS, etc. Also today the promo for the next schedule change (dubbed a Super Duper Late Winter Lineup) has a new slogan “You’re signed on to the CW4Kids” Slow and painful rebrand? 😒, If you’re not gonna change the bumpers untill Easter then why reveal your flashy new slogan now. Also the website (thecw4kids.com) STILL says this at the footer “© 2024 Warner Bros Discovery, Paramount Global, Nexstar Media Group” Hopefully it is updated to reflect 4Kids’s involvement soon. 
Thread Title: CW4Kids: A Slow Transition & The Future of Cartoons on the Block? 🔄📺

ToonMasterJay: So, has anyone else noticed how the CW4Kids transition is kinda like watching paint dry? The bumpers feel like Kids WB is haunting us. And they’re dropping their new slogan already, but the website footer is still all about the old guard… Mixed signals much?

DigitalToonFan: Yeah, the transition is definitely dragging its feet. I mean, if you’re going to bring in 4Kids, then do it with a bang! Not this half-Zoom half-real-life meeting vibe. The new lineup switches could be cool though, but I’ll miss Squish and The Amazing Adrenalini Brothers for sure.

GoGoFan223: Honestly, I’m most excited about GoGoRiki (or Smeshariki for the purists). It’s cute and has that potential to be big with the comedy crowd. Less about the action and more laughs—could be the perfect fit for the new direction they’re aiming for.

SonicXforever: Still processing the swap of Squish for Pokémon and TAAB for Sonic X… I mean, sure, classics! But Sonic X has been rerun how many times now? I guess they’re banking on that nostalgia factor to reel us in while they’re redecorating.

PokeNostalgia101: Not going to lie, I’m excited to have Pokémon Indigo League back—it’s like comfort food for my soul. But I hear you all, the change is weirdly slow. I get that it’s a big shift from Kids WB to full-on 4Kids ownership, yet you’d think they’d want to make their mark quick.

LyokoLover: Low-key glad Code Lyoko and Xiaolin Showdown are sticking around. They’ve got that unique appeal that isn’t easy to replace. But curious how long till that changes with this new “Super Duper Late Winter Lineup.” If they’re ushering out TTG and Scooby, who’s next?

Animanga4Life: Ultimate Muscle and Kirby: Right Back At Ya coming to the block brings back fond Saturday morning memories! Although, I do get why some are skeptical; a lot of these shows have seen multiple reruns over the years.

WebWader: I keep checking the website hoping it’s been updated… No dice. The weird blend of old and new branding isn’t helping anyone. At least update the footer to show 4Kids is steering the ship now. How hard can it be?

ComicStripKid: The lineup change feels like a balance between targeting the parents who grew up with these shows and trying to appeal to the new gen of cartoon fans. GoGoRiki’s charm and simplicity could be their ticket into the latter’s hearts. I see a mascot in the making.

ToonTalker91: Not thrilled about the slow pace here. It’s like we’re stuck in Kids WB limbo. I miss the days when a rebrand meant a fresh start, not this lingering goodbye. Curious how Gloopy and Glitter will fare with the rest of the changes looming… Will they be the last ones standing?

Admin_4KidsRevamp: Please bear with us during this transition. We’re working hard to bring a fresh and exciting lineup while honoring the legacy of what came before. Believe us, it’ll be worth the wait! Stay tuned for more updates and enjoy the shows in the meantime.

Thread Closed by Admin_4KidsRevamp
Write a follow up in mid Feb where the Feb lineup is here, 5 4Kids shows (Pokemon, Sonic, Ultimate Muscle, Kirby and GoGoRiki) and 5 Kids WB shows (Johnny Test, Xiaolin Showdown, Ultimate Spider Man, Gloopy and Glitter and Code Lyoko) Also, the website now has an account and points system, games, and has updated the footer to “© 2024 4Kids Entertainment”, Website otherwise remains the same, and still has show pages for removed shows, but this will slowly change in the coming weeks
Thread Title: CW4Kids Mid-Feb Lineup is Live! Balance of Old and New + Website Updates 🎮📺

DigitalToonFan: The balance has been struck, folks! Five 4Kids classics—Pokémon, Sonic, Ultimate Muscle, Kirby, and GoGoRiki—and five Kids WB staples—Johnny Test, Xiaolin Showdown, Ultimate Spider Man, Gloopy and Glitter, and Code Lyoko. It’s a mix of eras in one block!

ToonMasterJay: Checked out the CW4Kids website, they finally ditched the old footer. It’s official, this ship is sailing under the 4Kids flag now. Nice to see they’re stepping up their game with accounts and points, plus some games to boot!

Animanga4Life: 4Kids moving in with accounts and points? Sounds like they’re setting up a cool rewards system. And that might just get more kids invested in the website beyond streaming shows—a savvy move.

GoGoFan223: GoGoRiki getting prime time with the big 4Kids dogs is great news. Its cute factor is off the charts. Could be the one to round out the new vibe CW4Kids is going for if they play their cards right.

SonicXforever: Glad to see Sonic racing into the lineup. It’s about time he got back into the limelight. Now to see how he fares among the newer offerings. Talk about a speed battle!

PokeNostalgia101: Pokémon getting its rightful place has me all fired up! But it’s kinda odd to still see show pages for the ousted shows on the website. Anyone else notice that? Hopefully, they’ll clear that up soon.

LyokoLover: The fact that Code Lyoko is sticking around amidst all the changes shows its resilience. Maybe 4Kids recognizes the gold they have with some of these Kids WB shows. The perfect yin to their yang.

ComicStripKid: Well, can’t say 4Kids isn’t taking a balanced approach to this transition. Keeping half the lineup from Kids WB seems like a strategic way to ensure they don’t lose the existing audience while they woo new viewers with nostalgia and updated features.

WebWader: The website is getting there, slowly but surely. With account functionality and games, it looks like 4Kids is channeling some vintage interactivity magic. Here’s to mission: engage the digitally-savvy generation!

ToonTalker91: Seeing “© 2024 4Kids Entertainment” at the bottom of the website gives me mixed emotions—it’s good to see they’re updating stuff, but the nostalgia for Kids WB is real. Any bets on how long Gloopy and Glitter can hold out?

Admin_4KidsRevamp: Thanks for sticking with us through the changes! We’re excited about the new lineup and website features, and this is just the beginning. As for the site, we’re rolling out updates over the next few weeks to ensure the best experience for all our visitors.

Thread Closed by Admin_4KidsRevamp
Write a follow up where a new look with a digital online video style is coming to the block mid April, Also the slogan shown in the Feb promo is official, Also, 3 of the KWB shows are going, with CL and G&G staying, Also, Chaotic, TMNT: Back to the Sewer and Viva Pinata are joining the lineup, this also happens from mid April, Pokemon is also being swapped for Pokemon Horizons: The Series
Thread Title: CW4Kids New Digital Look & Lineup Shuffle – Mid-April Madness Incoming! 💥📺

ToonMasterJay: Mid-April’s gonna hit us with a serious 1-2 punch, folks! We’re diving into a digital online video style aesthetic, making the “You’re signed on to the CW4Kids” slogan legit. Plus, line-up’s getting shaken up again—big time!

DigitalToonFan: Heard the scoop that Johnny Test, Xiaolin Showdown, and Ultimate Spider Man are waving goodbye. But we’re keeping the virtual adventures of Code Lyoko and the quirky “Gloopy and Glitter”—two survivors of the cull.

Write an ad for the new lineup, and the character narrating it,

**Ad Start**

[Upbeat, adventurous music fades in, setting the scene for excitement and discovery.]

**Narrator: Sonic the Hedgehog** (Voice vibrant with enthusiasm, with the faintest hint of speed-tinged eagerness.)

**Sonic:** "Hey there, speedsters and cartoon connoisseurs! It's your pal Sonic here, zipping through to bring you the latest buzz from CW4Kids! We're revving up your Saturday mornings with a lineup that's more electrifying than a dash through Green Hill Zone!"

[Quick, dynamic shots of Pokémon Horizons: The Series, showcasing epic battles and new Pokémon.]

**Sonic:** "First up, journey into new realms with Pokémon Horizons: The Series. Ready for adventure? Because these Pokémon battles are just getting started!"

[Transition to high-energy snippets of GoGoRiki, with laughter and comic mishaps.]

**Sonic:** "Need a laugh? Dash over to GoGoRiki, where humor knows no bounds. It's wacky, it's fun, and it's definitely not your average walk in the park!"

[A brief, engaging peek into Four Eyes! follows, highlighting Emma's hilarious adjustments to earth.]

**Sonic:** "Prepare for out-of-this-world laughs with Four Eyes! Watch Emma navigate the wilds of fifth grade on Earth. Spoiler: It's an alien challenge like no other!"

[The intensity amps up with clips from Beyblade Burst Surge, Chaotic, and Kirby: Right Back at Ya! showcasing action and thrills.]

**Sonic:** "Crave some action? Spin into the world of Beyblade Burst Surge, get strategic with Chaotic, or bounce into adventure with Kirby: Right Back at Ya!. It’s all about the thrill of the battle and the power of friendship!"

[Softening the pace, lovely moments from Gloopy and Glitter and Will and Dewitt emphasize heartwarming lessons and fun.]

**Sonic:** "And for a touch of magic and heart—don’t miss Gloopy and Glitter and Will and Dewitt. It's where the wonders of friendship make every day extraordinary."

[Finally, a bold return to Sonic X, with Sonic winking at the audience.]

**Sonic:** "And you thought I’d forget? Hold onto your rings, because I’m bringing high-octane adventures directly to you with Sonic X. It's a wild ride, and you're all invited!"

[The music builds to a climax, with a montage of all the shows flashing by.]

**Sonic:** "So, tie up those laces tight, 'cause CW4Kids' new lineup is here to take your weekends to the next level! Join me, Sonic, and all my friends for the ultimate Saturday morning experience. Don't blink, or you might just miss it!"

[The ad wraps up with a flashy display of the CW4Kids logo and the tagline splashing across the screen.]

**Sonic:** "Zoom into action, laugh until it hurts, and embark on journeys to new worlds, only on CW4Kids. See ya there!"

[Upbeat music fades out.]

**Ad End**

Write a blog post saying Goodbye G&G, hello W&D

### Goodbye Gloopy & Glitter, Hello Will & DeWitt: A New Chapter Begins at CW4Kids

In the ever-evolving landscape of children's television, change is the only constant. As we bid farewell to the cosmic adventures of our beloved alien buddies, Gloopy and Glitter, CW4Kids invites viewers to welcome a new duo that promises to fill our Saturday mornings with laughter, learning, and endless fun. So, here's to turning the page: Goodbye G&G, hello Will & DeWitt.

**A Fond Farewell to Gloopy & Glitter**

Gloopy and Glitter came into our lives as shining examples of curiosity, friendship, and the joy of discovering the world around us. Whether it was navigating the intricacies of human customs or embarking on hilariously heartwarming misadventures, G&G taught us to embrace our differences and to see the world through a lens of wonder and empathy. Their time on CW4Kids may have come to an end, but the memories and lessons they've left behind will continue to glow brightly in the hearts of their fans.

**Embracing the New: Will & DeWitt**

As the curtains close on one chapter, they open on another that's brimming with potential. Enter Will and his amphibian friend, DeWitt, a pair whose bond transcends the ordinary to explore the extraordinary in everyday life. Will, with his ever-curious mind and kind heart, together with DeWitt, the frog who's more than willing to lend a helping hand (or webbed foot), are set to take us on journeys filled with imagination, problem-solving, and the power of positive thinking.

What sets Will & DeWitt apart is not just their vibrant world filled with colorful characters and engaging stories but also the duo's dynamic approach to overcoming challenges. Whether it's facing fears, conquering doubts, or learning new things, Will and DeWitt show us that with a bit of creativity and encouragement from friends, there's nothing we can't achieve.

**Why This Change Matters**

The transition from Gloopy and Glitter to Will & DeWitt is more than just a programming change; it reflects CW4Kids' commitment to diversifying its content to resonate with its young audience's evolving interests and needs. In Will & DeWitt, viewers will find relatable themes of friendship, courage, and the value of having a positive outlook—messages that are as important as ever in today's world.

As we embrace Will & DeWitt into the CW4Kids family, let's remember the laughter and lessons G&G have shared with us. Here's to new adventures, new friends, and the continued joy of Saturday mornings filled with fun and learning.

In the end, the essence of children's programming lies not in the characters or the settings but in the stories told and the values imparted. Gloopy and Glitter have passed the baton to Will & DeWitt, who, with open hearts and wide-eyed wonder, are ready to write the next chapter of engaging and enriching entertainment on CW4Kids.

So, gather around, kids and kids-at-heart, because our Saturday mornings are about to get even more exciting. Goodbye, Gloopy & Glitter; you'll be missed but not forgotten. Hello, Will & DeWitt; we can't wait to see where you'll take us next. Here's to discovering the magic in the mundane and finding friends in unexpected places.

**Stay tuned, because the adventure is just beginning.**
